steps.title: Find the subject of the blank
steps.content: The sentence reads "The map ______ the area of a plot of land, shows a river valley, and includes the cardinal directions." The subject is "The map" — singular, third person.

steps.title: Match the verb to the parallel series
steps.content: The blank is the first verb in a three-part compound predicate joined with "shows" and "includes," both simple present, third-person singular. The blank must take the same form to keep subject-verb agreement and parallelism.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'describes': third-person singular present, agrees with "map" and parallels "shows"/"includes." ✓
  • Choice B — 'describe': plural form — violates subject-verb agreement with singular "map." ✗
  • Choice C — 'have described': plural auxiliary and wrong tense — breaks both agreement and parallelism with the present-tense series. ✗
  • Choice D — 'are describing': plural auxiliary and progressive aspect — violates agreement and parallelism. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
